title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Mastery Check
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar profiling, allocator performance, and hardening decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for measured allocator optimization report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Allocation Telemetry · changed case · Quiz Allocation Telemetry
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 5755. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
